items.title: Finding the untested error path
items.scenario: Your `call_api` has a 429 retry branch you've never tested.
items.explanation: Run `pytest --cov=ragapp --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing`. The `Missing` column points at line 47 — the 429 branch. Add a test, coverage rises, real bug surface vanishes.

items.title: Diff coverage on a PR
items.scenario: A teammate's PR adds 200 lines of code but only 60 are tested.
items.explanation: Tools like `diff-cover` show coverage *of the diff only*. PR comment: 'only 30% of changed lines are tested.' The conversation now happens before the bug ships.

items.title: Excluding a debug helper
items.scenario: `debug_dump()` is a helper used only when manually debugging; you don't want to test it.
items.explanation: Add `# pragma: no cover` to the function. Coverage tool ignores it. Document *why* it's excluded in a comment so future engineers know it wasn't an oversight.

items.title: Critical module gate, lax elsewhere
items.scenario: You want strict coverage on `ragapp.retry` but not on `ragapp.cli`.
items.explanation: Per-module fail-under in `pyproject.toml`: `retry` requires 95% branch, `cli` requires 60% line. Use module-level rules; a single global number is too blunt.

items.title: Spotting the coverage cliff after a refactor
items.scenario: A refactor moved logic out of `chunker.py` into a new `splitter.py`; coverage drops 8 points.
items.explanation: The drop is the tests no longer following the moved code. Either re-run the same tests against the new module path or update test imports — coverage trend in CI made the issue visible immediately.
